title: The One With All The Cheesecakes

lines.text: Chandler and Monica's, Chandler is sitting at the kitchen table eating cheesecake. The box it came in is also on the table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh. Mmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Comes in the front door and walks towards the kitchen.) Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you have got to try this cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y'know I'm not that much of a sweet tooth. I-(Chandler puts a forkful of the cheesecake in her mouth.)-Wow. My God, so creamy. Oh my God, this is the best cheesecake I have ever had. Where did you get this? (She reaches over to look at the label on the box.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nervously) It was at the front door. When I got home. Somebody sent it to us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, this is not addressed to you. This is addressed to Mrs. Braverman downstairs. (Gasping) Thief.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I-no! I didn't read the box before I opened it. And you can't return a box after you've opened the box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why, why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because it's too delicious.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, you stole this cheesecake. That is wrong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no! It is going to be okay, because Mrs. Braverman is gonna send away for a free one and that way we all win! The only losers are the big cheesecake conglomerate, (Reading the label) Momma's Little Bakery. (Pause) I feel terrible, I'm a horrible, horrible, horrible person.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking a bite) Oh, I'm sorry what?
Opening Credits
Central Perk, everyone except Rachel is there as Joey gets up and starts putting on his coat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I should get going, big day a work. Y'know I'm in a coma? Today, they do this test on me and it turns out I'm not brain dead.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah-ah-ah Mr. Smartie Pants, it's just not my character that's not brain dead. Hey, so Pheebs, we still on for tonight?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'll see you at 8:00.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Joey exits.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, what's at 8:00?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I have dinner plans with Joey. We get together about once a month to discuss the rest of you guys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, did not know that! May I say how lovely you look today?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Duly noted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. (Phoebe gets up to get a refill.) (To Monica) Oh! So for tomorrow, do you want to rent a car and drive down together or what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cousin Frannie's wedding, its tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You were invited?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My God, I can't believe this! I mean I knew that mom and dad were invited, but I thought that was it! I mean from the ages 7 to 9 Frannie and I were inseparable!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, maybe since the age of 9, Frannie's made some new friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well l-look okay, it's probably just a mistake. Let me call Aunt Sheryl okay? Maybe you are invited and the invitation just got lost in the mail.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well you call her and tell her that y'know when we were kids her precious little Frannie tried to undress me several times, okay? And if I hadn't have stopped her, there probably wouldn't even be a wedding to go too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, she tried to undress me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I used to undress my cousin Glenn. (Monica looks at him then sushes him.)
Joey and Rachel's, Monica, Chandler, Rachel, and Ross are playing Monopoly as Phoebe enters angrily.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joseph Francis Tribbiani are you home yet?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, I think he's still out. What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'll tell you Rachel Karen Green, I had plans with Joey tonight and he left me this note. (Hands it to Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (reading the note) "Pheebs, can't make it, got a date. Talk to you later. Big Daddy." (Laughs) Big Daddy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that's a nickname we were trying out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know what nickname never caught on? The Ross-A-Tron! (Monica shakes her head in disgust.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Here's Joseph Francis!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-Wha-Ho! What are you middle naming me for?! I left you a note!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what?! That doesn't give you the right to ditch me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you can cancel plans with friends if there is the possibility for sex!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe he's right, that is the rule.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't accept this rule. When me make plans, I expect you to show up. Okay, I can't just be a way to kill time 'til you meet someone better! Y'know boyfriends and girlfriends come and go, but this (Motions that their friendship) is for life!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! I'm so sorry; I had no idea it would bother you this much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it does.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, can I-can I make it up to you? Huh? I'm sorry. (They hug.) How about uh, dinner tomorrow night?! I'll pay for myself!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you wore me down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Joe, while you're over there how about another beer for the Ross-A-Tron?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Ross-Is that back?!! (Chandler motions no.)
Monica and Chandler's, Rachel enters to find Chandler staring at another cheesecake box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Another cheesecake came! They delivered it to the wrong address again!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So just bring it back downstairs, what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't seem to say goodbye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you serious?! Chandler, we ate an entire cheesecake two days ago and you want more?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I've forgotten what it tastes like okay?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was cheesecake. It was fine. It had a buttery, crumbly, graham cracker crust, with a very rich yet light, cream cheese filling... (Pause) Wow! My whole mouth just filled with saliva!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (closing the box) Y'know what? Forget it! We are just hungry! We have not had lunch! We are just light-headed! So let us go out and have lunch and forget about the cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and we'll drop it off downstairs so that we're not tempted.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good idea. Where do you want to go to lunch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Momma's Little Bakery, Chicago, Illinois. (They exit with the cheesecake.)
Central Perk, Monica and Joey are there. Joey is reading a newspaper.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Awww! Mel Torme died.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, that paper's like a year old!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aw! Does that mean the Sam Goody's sale is over?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I finally heard back from Aunt Sheryl and apparently it wasn't a mistake. Ahh, there's-there's limited seating in the hall.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Limited seating?! (Screechingly) I am just one tiny person!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well yeah, but she doesn't know that. I mean, the last time she saw you-you would've turned one of those little wedding chairs into kindling.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (disgusted) Limited seating! Oh, that is such a lame excuse! That's not the reason she's not inviting me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what's the big deal?! I wasn't even invited to the ceremony, just the reception. And-and y'know what? If it makes you feel any better, Joan and I will just make an appearance and then, and then we'll-we'll leave early as a sign of protest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joan?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, Joan Tedeski my date. She's an assistant professor in the Linguistics department. Tall, very beautiful, and despite what some people say, not broad backed!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, you got Ross Gellar and guest?! I wasn't invited and you got "and guest?!"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-uh, excuse me, I do have to interrupt on Ross's behalf. I-I think the rule applies here y'know, since she has a chance to get on broad back...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupting) Not broad backed!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, y'know, you're bringing me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? I can't cancel on Joan!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bec-Did you not hear me?! She's an assistant professor in the Linguistics department, okay? They're wild! Why do you want to come anyway?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because! She's my cousin. I mean, we grew up together! We're family y'know? Well that's important to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, all right, I'll take you. I'll go call Joan. (Does so.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww that's nice. Family should be there, huh? This is her wedding, happiest day of her life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) We'll see.
The Lobby of Chandler and Rachel's building, Chandler and Rachel are returning from lunch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, thank you for lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wait a minute, I didn't pay, I thought you paid!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So apparently we just don't pay for food anymore. (Rachel laughs then Chandler notices something.) Do you see what I see?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Its still there! (The cheesecake they returned to Mrs. Braverman is still lying in front of her door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mrs. Braverman must be out. (They move closer to it.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She could be out of town. Maybe she'll be gone for months.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: By then, the cheesecake may have gone bad. We don't want her to come back to bad cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No that could kill her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we don't want that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No so we're protecting her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we should take it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But we should move quick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because I think I just heard her moving around in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! (Rachel grabs the cheesecake and they take off upstairs.)
Central Perk, Monica and Phoebe are entering. As Phoebe is sitting down, she recognizes someone sitting at the counter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) (whispering) Oh my God! That's David!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: David who?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David the scientist guy, David that I was in love with, David who went to Russia and broke my heart David!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (noticing Phoebe) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you say someone's name enough, they turn around.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David! (He kisses her cheek) What-what are you doing here? Aren't you supposed to be in Russia?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I'm just, I'm just in town for a conference. Umm, God you look phenomenal!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well... Yeah. You look great too. Did you get a haircut?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah. Well I-I got like thirty of them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Umm, look I-I-I got a confession to make...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, I-I-I was hoping to run into you here. I didn't know whether I should call or not, y'know I-I was only in town for a few days. And y'know, I didn't want to intrude on your life or-or anything like that, but I-I really wanted to see you and-but I didn't know if you wanted to see me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, of course I would want to see you. I...I think about you all the time.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Really? Because I think about you all the time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I mean, there's a statue in Minsk...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That reminds me of you so much, I mean umm, it-it's actually of Lenin. But, y'know at certain angles...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Umm, anyway...Do you want to have dinner tonight?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (excited) Yes! Oh no!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh. Uh, what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't. I can't believe I have plans, I can't. Can you do it tomorrow night though?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh no, I have to go in a few hours. I have to be on the red-eye. Well listen, y'know, next time you're in Minsk umm...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh. (They go over and talk.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I have plans with Joey tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So! He'll understand!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No he won't. And that's not even the point! Monica, I made a whole speech about you do not cancel plans with friends! And now y'know what? Just because, potentially, the love of my life comes back from Russia just for one night, I-I should change my beliefs?! I should change beliefs! No! No! No, if I don't have my principles, I don't have anything!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, you are so strong.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or! I should rush through dinner with Joey and I can meet David at 9:00!
Commercial Break
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Chandler are eating the cheesecake right out of the box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking a bite) Oh my God! That is so good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm full, and yet I know if I stop eating this, I'll regret it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (seeing what they're doing) What do you got there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it's umm, it's tofu cake. Do you want some? (He makes a disgusted noise and heads for his room, Chandler follows him in.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh? Uh... (He starts taking off his pants.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dude! Dude! (Motions that Joey should pull up his pants.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! (Pulls up his pants.) Sorry. Uh, I've got those plans with Phoebe, why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh really? Uh, Monica said she had a date at 9:00.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Tonight?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what Monica said.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: After she gave me that big speech?! She goes and makes a date with a guy on the same night she has plans with me? I think she's trying to pull a fast one on Big Daddy!
Cousin Frannie's Wedding Reception, Monica and Ross are entering and finding their table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh here, I think this is us. (Reading the name cards.) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Limited seating my ass. Let's see who made the cut. (To the couple sitting to her right.) Hi!

lines.speaker: The Wedding Guest
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm Monica Gellar. Who do you know the bride and groom?

lines.speaker: The Wedding Guest
lines.text: Oh, I used to work with Frannie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Used to work with her. Used to! I'm a relative and I didn't get invited! A blood relative! Blood!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Monica) Stop saying, "Blood" to strangers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to the couple on her left) So, how about you huh? How do you know the happy couple?

lines.speaker: The Second Guest
lines.text: We went to college with both of them and now we live next door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you're fine.
Iridium Restaurant, Phoebe and Joey are looking at the menus as the waiter comes to take their order.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Are you guys ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! I will have the green salad, umm the house salad, and water's fine.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: (To Joey) And for you sir?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, this slow roasted salmon, just how slow are we talkin' here?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: It's uh, it's already been roasted.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, then no. Maybe I should hear those specials again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Joey, we've heard the specials three times! Okay? There's prime rib, mahi mahi, and a very special lobster ravioli. (She grabs his menu and hands it to the waiter.)

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Actually we're out of the lobster ravioli. (Putting Joey's menu under his arm.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, that changes everything! (Grabs his menu and starts looking at it again. The waiter leaves.) Y'know what Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You were right before. I mean, friends are so important.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (checking her watch) Yeah, I'm very wise. I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know-y'know what I really want?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is to have a long, long talk. Y'know? Get Joey out on the open road and really open him up.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: (returning) Any progress?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! I will have the lobster ravioli.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God Joey, this is taking forever!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the rush? What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: W-w-I just-it's that-I have-y'know I have-I have an appointment. And it's very important.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well... It's a date.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A date?! No, no Pheebs you-you must be mistaken, because I know you wouldn't schedule a date on the same night you have plans with a friend!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on Joey, don't make me feel badly about this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm gonna!! That's right! Yeah, you made me feel really guilty about goin' out with that girl! Like-like-like I did something terrible to you! And now Pheebs, you're doing the same thing!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That-It's not the same thing! This is totally different! This is with David! Remember David, the scientist guy? Okay, he's very special to me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, well my girl from the other night was special. She was a scientist too!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She was?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, she graduated from high school!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, whatever. Y'know what? I don't have time have time to convince you because he's only here for four hours, and I'm gonna go see him! (Gets up and leaves.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! (She exits.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the waiter) What are you still doin' here?! I told you, lobster ravioli!
Outside Central Perk, Phoebe is running up to meet David.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hey! Oh, I was just about to leave. I-I-I-I didn't think you were coming.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I wouldn't miss this.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, I'm very glad you're here. (Kisses her hand.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're such a gentleman. (Grabs his arm.) Come on! We're going to my place! (Drags him off to her place.)
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is eating the cheesecake and Chandler enters and catches her in the act.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you eating the cheesecake without me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (with a mouthful) Mm-mmm. (Nods no.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I will give you a hundred dollars to whistle right now. (She tries to whistle and blows little chunks of cheesecake out of her mouth.) How can you eat the cheesecake without me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, what are you going to do?! Are you gonna go run tell Monica?! Are you gonna tell Joey?! No! Because then you will have to tell them what we did! We are desert stealers! We are living outside the law!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what? I don't trust you with this cake anymore! And I got it first, and I'm takin' it back! (Grabs the cheesecake and heads for his apartment.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no-no, no you don't!
[Cut to Chandler and Monica's as they enter.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes! Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You think I trust you with it?! No! We're gonna split it! You take half and I take half!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that's not fair, you've already had some!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Oh, well then y'know what? I think Monica would be very interested to know that you called her cheesecake dry and mealy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do we use to split it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! (Grabs a knife and cuts it in half.) All right, pick a half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (examining the cake) Okay well, this side looks bigger. Uh... There's more crust on this side. Y'know? So, maybe if I measured...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh for God sake just pick a piece!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, I'll pick that one. (Points.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's also the smaller piece. (Puts the piece onto a plate.) Okay, there you go. Enjoy your half my friend, but that is it. No sharing. No switching, and don't come crying to me if you eat your piece to fast. (As she's saying that she is backing out the door, when she finishes she turns around to return to her place, stumbles and drops the cheesecake on the floor.) Oh!!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (gloatingly and holding his piece) Ohhh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you gotta give me some of your piece.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho-ho-no! No! No switching! No sharing, and don't come crying to me! Ha-ha-ha! I may just sit here and have my cake all day! Just sit here in the hallway and eat my... (Rachel knocks the plate from his hand and it falls on the floor. That process leaves just the forkful Chandler has, Rachel starts to go after that little bit and Chandler retreats into his apartment.)
Cousin Frannie's Wedding Reception, Monica and Ross are sitting at the table, alone as a woman approaches.

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: Ross, sweetheart!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, hey Aunt Millie.

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Isn't it a beautiful wedding?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, yes it is. It's uh... (Aunt Millie uses this opportunity to grab Ross and kiss him on the lips. After she leaves Ross quickly wipes his mouth with a napkin.) Every time on the lips! Why?! Why on lips?!
Cousin Frannie and her husband enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here's Frannie. Hmm, won't she be happy to see me? (Starts to get up and great Frannie.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now wait a minute, you be nice! All right? I didn't bring you here so you can ambush her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Frannie was the one who found your Playboys and showed them to mom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That bitch! (He gets up and they go over to ambush Frannie. Monica taps on Frannie's shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.text: (turning around) Monica! What...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Am I doing here? Why? Surprised to see me? Ross brought me. How do you like that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Frannie) Hi Frannie, congratulations.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You invite my brother, you invite my whole family, and not me?! Why?! What-Why wouldn't you want me at your wedding? What could I have possibly done?! (Frannie's husband walks up.) Stuart!

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.text: I believe you know my husband.
Monica is shocked into silence.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So it's really a question of who could you have possibly done.
Outside Central Perk, David has a cab waiting to take him to the airport and he's saying goodbye to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, I hate this but I-I-I have to go. I-I can't miss my flight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure? I'll bet there's another flight to Minsk in like...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: July. Umm, (He speaks Russian.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's really beautiful. What does it mean?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Please, clean my beakers. I don't get out of the lab much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's good. I got to admit, I thought it was something else.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I... Well I really actually wanted to say umm, that, but um, I figured I probably shouldn't because y'know, I...have to leave.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're right! You're right. Don't say it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I-I do though.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do too. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Bye Phoebe. (He gets in and the cab drives off. Joey walks up and witnesses that event.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (seeing him) Okay, now's not the time Joey. All right? You can yell at me tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No! No Pheebs, I'm not gonna yell at you. I just y'know, started thinking about you and David and I...remember how bummed you were the first time he left. And I just... Oh Pheebs, come here. (He hugs her.) Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I'm not okay. The only guy I've ever been crazy about has gone to Minsk and I may never...I may never see him again. (Crying.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y'know you could always visit him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh right, like they're gonna let me have a passport.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anything I can do? Whatever you need.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well-But-Now, if-if you can achieve positronic distillation of sub-atomic particles y'know before he does, then he can come back. (They hug again.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can give it a shot.
The Hallway, Chandler and Rachel are on their knees with forks trying to salvage what they can of the cheesecake off of the floor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Yay! Look! There's a piece that doesn't have floor on it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stick to your side!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, come on now!
Joey finishes climbing the stairs and sees them. Chandler and Rachel both stop and look up at him. Joey sits down on the step.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pulls out a fork) All right, what are we havin'? (Starts digging in.)
Ending Credits
Cousin Frannie's Wedding Reception, Ross and Monica are at the door and about to leave.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wait I forgot my wrap.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Oh, okay. Wait here. (Goes to get it, but before he gets there Aunt Millie sits down on it forcing him to pull it out from behind her which gets her attention.)

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Hi sweetie! Are you leaving?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Give us a kiss. Come on! Come on!
Ross hesitates then leans down trying to get her to kiss his cheek, but she moves his head around and kisses him on the lips again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?! Why on the lips?! (He wipes his mouth on Monica's wrap and walks off, leaving Aunt Millie stunned.)
End
